diagnostics.consequence: Automated review can miss issues that require author interaction or direct figure inspection, so reviewer outputs are incomplete as a sole decision basis.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.

diagnostics.statement: The implemented automated reviewer lacks rebuttal-question capability and direct vision-based figure inspection.

diagnostics.consequence: Generated papers may be over-trusted if used as validated scientific findings rather than leads requiring follow-up.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

diagnostics.statement: The source warns that the generated scientific content should not be accepted at face value.

diagnostics.consequence: Adding more automated reviews and a meta-aggregation layer may consume resources without improving measured reviewer decision performance.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.

diagnostics.statement: Five-review ensembling with meta-aggregation did not improve the automated reviewer’s measured performance.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: 100% of generated-paper claims that are surfaced as findings have documented human approval or explicit labeling as unvalidated hints before external use.
success_check.evidence_needed: Audit log or publication workflow records showing labeling and approval status for each generated paper or claim.
success_check.observable: Generated scientific claims are gated by human approval or clearly labeled as follow-up hints.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: No final accept/reject or release decision is made solely from automated-review output when figures or rebuttal-dependent claims are present.
success_check.evidence_needed: Decision policy plus sample decision traces showing automated reviewer output is advisory and that figure/rebuttal checks are handled separately.
success_check.observable: Automated reviewer output is used as a diagnostic signal rather than a standalone gate.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: To evaluate the LLM-based reviewer’s performance, we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).
Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: A new reviewer configuration is adopted only if it shows a statistically or pre-specified practically meaningful improvement over the single-review baseline on held-out real-paper decisions.
success_check.evidence_needed: Held-out evaluation report with baseline, candidate configuration, decision metrics, variance estimates, and adoption criterion.
success_check.observable: Reviewer-aggregation changes are evaluated against real conference-paper decision data before adoption.

memo: Implemented mechanisms include prior-literature filtering after idea generation, iterative experiment replanning conditioned on completed results, automated reviewer evaluation against 500 ICLR 2022 OpenReview papers, and archiving completed ideas plus reviewer feedback across repetitions. The main architecture risks are that generated scientific content is explicitly not validated at face value, the reviewer cannot ask rebuttal questions or inspect figures directly, and ensembling/meta-aggregation did not improve reviewer performance.
